title: myosotis scorpioides subsp. repens
intro: Myosotis scorpioides subsp. repens, also known as creeping forget-me-not, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the family Boraginaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of creeping forget-me-not include its potential to reduce inflammation, relieve digestive issues, and act as an antiseptic to promote wound healing. Creeping forget-me-not can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used in traditional folk medicine for its soothing and calming effects.
